channel.title: NHC Eastern North Pacific
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Eastern North Pacific

item.title: Eastern North Pacific Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABPZ20 KNHC 192311
TWOEP

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
500 PM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026

For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Elida, located well west of the southern tip of the Baja
California Peninsula, and on Tropical Storm Fausto, located
several hundred miles southwest of southwestern Mexico.

Eastern and Central East Pacific:
A broad area of low pressure is forecast to develop well south of
Mexico during the latter part of the week. Environmental conditions
appear conducive for development of the system thereafter, and a
tropical depression could form while it moves westward to
west-northwestward across the eastern and central portions of the
eastern Pacific.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...medium...50 percent.

South-Southwest of Hawaii:
Showers and thunderstorms associated with a trough of low pressure
located well south of the Hawaiian Islands remain disorganized.
Environmental conditions appear conducive for some gradual
development of this system as it moves generally westward during
the next several days.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...low...20 percent.

&&

Public Advisories on Tropical Storm Fausto are issued under
WMO header WTPZ31 KNHC and under AWIPS header MIATCPEP1.
Forecast/Advisories on Tropical Storm Fausto are issued under
WMO header WTPZ21 KNHC and under AWIPS header MIATCMEP1.

$$
Forecaster Gibbs
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Fausto (EP1/EP062026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...FAUSTO STEADILY STRENGTHENING... As of 8:00 PM MST Sun Jul 19 the center of Fausto was located near 12.6, -114.7 with movement NW at 12 mph. The minimum central pressure was 1002 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 45 mph.

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Public Advisory Number 5
item.description:
Issued at 800 PM MST Sun Jul 19 2026 
000
WTPZ31 KNHC 200231
TCPEP1
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Fausto Advisory Number   5
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
800 PM MST Sun Jul 19 2026
 
...FAUSTO STEADILY STRENGTHENING...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 800 PM MST...0300 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...12.6N 114.7W
ABOUT 780 MI...1250 KM SSW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...45 MPH...75 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...NW OR 310 DEGREES AT 12 MPH...19 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1002 MB...29.59 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 800 PM MST (0300 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Fausto was
located near latitude 12.6 North, longitude 114.7 West. Fausto is
moving toward the northwest near 12 mph (19 km/h), and this general 
motion with a gradual turn toward the west-northwest is expected 
through Tuesday. The cyclone is forecast to slow somewhat through 
Wednesday before gradually increasing its forward speed later in the 
week.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 45 mph (75 km/h) with higher gusts.
Steady strengthening followed by a period of rapid intensification 
is forecast during the next couple of days, and Fausto is forecast 
to become a hurricane by Monday night.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 140 miles (220 km)
from the center.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 1002 mb (29.59 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
None.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 200 AM MST.
 
$$
Forecaster Gibbs (CPHC)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Forecast Advisory Number 5
item.description:
Issued at 0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
WTPZ21 KNHC 200231
TCMEP1
 
TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER   5
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026
0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 12.6N 114.7W AT 20/0300Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  40 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE NORTHWEST OR 310 DEGREES AT  10 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE 1002 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  40 KT WITH GUSTS TO  50 KT.
34 KT....... 30NE  20SE 120SW  80NW.
4 M SEAS.... 90NE  60SE   0SW  60NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 12.6N 114.7W AT 20/0300Z
AT 20/0000Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 12.3N 114.3W
 
FORECAST VALID 20/1200Z 13.3N 116.1W
MAX WIND  50 KT...GUSTS  60 KT.
50 KT... 20NE  20SE   0SW   0NW.
34 KT... 60NE  40SE 130SW  80NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/0000Z 14.5N 117.7W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
64 KT... 20NE  10SE   0SW   0NW.
50 KT... 40NE  30SE   0SW  20NW.
34 KT... 80NE  70SE 130SW 100NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/1200Z 15.6N 119.4W
MAX WIND  80 KT...GUSTS 100 KT.
64 KT... 25NE  20SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT... 50NE  40SE  30SW  40NW.
34 KT...100NE 100SE 110SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0000Z 16.3N 120.8W
MAX WIND  90 KT...GUSTS 110 KT.
64 KT... 35NE  25SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...110NE 100SE 110SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1200Z 16.7N 122.3W
MAX WIND  95 KT...GUSTS 115 KT.
64 KT... 40NE  30SE  20SW  35NW.
50 KT... 70NE  60SE  50SW  60NW.
34 KT...140NE 130SE 110SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0000Z 17.1N 124.0W
MAX WIND  90 KT...GUSTS 110 KT.
64 KT... 40NE  30SE  20SW  35NW.
50 KT... 70NE  50SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...140NE 130SE 110SW 130NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 24/0000Z 17.7N 128.8W
MAX WIND  85 KT...GUSTS 105 KT.
50 KT... 80NE  60SE  50SW  70NW.
34 KT...160NE 140SE 110SW 150NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 25/0000Z 18.5N 134.1W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
50 KT... 70NE  50SE  40SW  70NW.
34 KT...160NE 140SE 110SW 160NW.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 12.6N 114.7W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 20/0900Z
 
$$
FORECASTER GIBBS (CPHC)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Forecast Discussion Number 5
item.description:
Issued at 800 PM MST Sun Jul 19 2026 
000
WTPZ41 KNHC 200234
TCDEP1
 
Tropical Storm Fausto Discussion Number   5
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
800 PM MST Sun Jul 19 2026
 
Satellite imagery indicates that Fausto's convective organization 
has continued to improve during the past several hours. Deep 
convection has become better established near the center, with a
well-defined upper-level outflow over the southern semicircle. 
Subjective Dvorak estimates from TAFB are T3.0/45 kt, while 
objective UW-CIMSS intensity estimates range from 30 to 40 kt. Given 
the improving satellite presentation and a blend of these estimates, 
the initial intensity is increased to 40 kt.

The initial motion estimate is toward the northwest, or 310/10 kt. 
While there remains some uncertainty in the exact position of the 
low-level center, track guidance remains in good agreement through 
about 96 h, with the cross-track spread increasing somewhat 
thereafter. A subtropical ridge is forecast to build westward in the 
wake of Elida, steering Fausto on a west-northwestward track during 
the next couple of days. Thereafter, the system is expected to 
gradually slow and turn slightly more westward through Wednesday 
before resuming a west-northwestward motion while accelerating later 
in the forecast period. The NHC track forecast is very similar to 
the previous advisory, but has been nudged slightly to the right to 
better align with the multi-model consensus (HCCA) and the Google 
DeepMind ensemble mean.

Environmental conditions are forecast to remain conducive for 
strengthening during the next few days, with warm sea surface 
temperatures, deep moisture, and minimal vertical wind shear 
expected to persist along Fausto's forecast track. Accordingly, 
Fausto is forecast to become a hurricane within 24 h, and a period 
of rapid intensification appears likely during the next couple of 
days. Fausto is forecast to reach its peak intensity by midweek. 
Thereafter, guidance suggest that decreasing upper-level divergence 
and a somewhat drier mid-level environment should induce a gradual 
weakening trend. The latest NHC intensity forecast remains near the 
middle of the guidance envelope through about 60 h, then lies near 
the upper end of the guidance envelope thereafter.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  20/0300Z 12.6N 114.7W   40 KT  45 MPH
 12H  20/1200Z 13.3N 116.1W   50 KT  60 MPH
 24H  21/0000Z 14.5N 117.7W   65 KT  75 MPH
 36H  21/1200Z 15.6N 119.4W   80 KT  90 MPH
 48H  22/0000Z 16.3N 120.8W   90 KT 105 MPH
 60H  22/1200Z 16.7N 122.3W   95 KT 110 MPH
 72H  23/0000Z 17.1N 124.0W   90 KT 105 MPH
 96H  24/0000Z 17.7N 128.8W   85 KT 100 MPH
120H  25/0000Z 18.5N 134.1W   75 KT  85 MPH
 
$$
Forecaster Gibbs (CPHC)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Wind Speed Probabilities Number 5
item.description:
Issued at 0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
FOPZ11 KNHC 200232
PWSEP1
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER   5           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026               
0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 0300Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 12.6 NORTH...LONGITUDE 114.7 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED   
WINDS NEAR 40 KTS...45 MPH...75 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        00Z MON AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       00Z MON 12Z MON 00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 00Z THU 00Z FRI
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             12Z MON 00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 00Z THU 00Z FRI 00Z SAT
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
15N 120W       34  X   1( 1)  68(69)   7(76)   1(77)   X(77)   X(77)
15N 120W       50  X   X( X)  30(30)   8(38)   1(39)   X(39)   X(39)
15N 120W       64  X   X( X)  13(13)   7(20)   X(20)   X(20)   X(20)
 
15N 125W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   8( 8)   6(14)   X(14)
15N 125W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   1( 3)   X( 3)
15N 125W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   1( 2)   X( 2)
 
20N 125W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  10(10)  11(21)   1(22)
20N 125W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   2( 3)   X( 3)
20N 125W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   X( 1)
 
15N 130W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)   4(10)
15N 130W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   1( 2)
15N 130W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)
 
20N 130W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  25(25)  16(41)
20N 130W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)   8(14)
20N 130W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   3( 5)
 
15N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)
 
20N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  42(42)
20N 135W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  16(16)
20N 135W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   7( 7)
 
20N 140W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER GIBBS (CPHC)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Elida (EP5/EP052026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...ELIDA BEGINS TO ACCELERATE TOWARD COLDER WATERS... As of 8:00 PM PDT Sun Jul 19 the center of Elida was located near 24.1, -126.8 with movement NNW at 12 mph. The minimum central pressure was 996 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 50 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Elida
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...ELIDA BEGINS TO ACCELERATE TOWARD COLDER WATERS...

item.title: Tropical Storm Elida Public Advisory Number 22
item.description:
Issued at 800 PM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026 
000
WTPZ35 KNHC 200240
TCPEP5
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Elida Advisory Number  22
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP052026
800 PM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026
 
...ELIDA BEGINS TO ACCELERATE TOWARD COLDER WATERS...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 800 PM PDT...0300 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...24.1N 126.8W
ABOUT 1075 MI...1725 KM W OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...50 MPH...85 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...NNW OR 335 DEGREES AT 12 MPH...19 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...996 MB...29.42 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 800 PM PDT (0300 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Elida was
located near latitude 24.1 North, longitude 126.8 West. Elida is
moving toward the north-northwest near 12 mph (19 km/h). A turn 
toward the north at a slightly faster speed is expected tonight 
through Monday. The system is then expected to continue northward 
but gradually slow down over the following day or two.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 50 mph (85 km/h) with higher gusts.
Gradual weakening is anticipated. Elida is forecast to become a 
remnant low late Monday or early Tuesday.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 175 miles (280 km)
from the center.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 996 mb (29.42 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF:  Swells generated by Elida will affect portions of the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula and southern California
during the next several days. These swells will likely result in
life-threatening surf and rip current conditions. Please consult
products from your local weather office.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 200 AM PDT.
 
$$
Forecaster D. Zelinsky
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Elida Forecast Advisory Number 22
item.description:
Issued at 0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
WTPZ25 KNHC 200240
TCMEP5
 
TROPICAL STORM ELIDA FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  22
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP052026
0300 UTC MON JUL 20 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 24.1N 126.8W AT 20/0300Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE NORTH-NORTHWEST OR 335 DEGREES AT  10 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  996 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  45 KT WITH GUSTS TO  55 KT.
34 KT.......150NE 110SE  60SW 150NW.
4 M SEAS....240NE 180SE 300SW 270NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 24.1N 126.8W AT 20/0300Z
AT 20/0000Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 23.5N 126.5W
 
FORECAST VALID 20/1200Z 26.0N 127.3W
MAX WIND  40 KT...GUSTS  50 KT.
34 KT...100NE  70SE  30SW 100NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/0000Z 28.7N 127.6W
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT... 60NE  40SE   0SW  50NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/1200Z 31.6N 127.7W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  30 KT...GUSTS  40 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0000Z 34.2N 127.6W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  25 KT...GUSTS  35 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1200Z 35.6N 127.4W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  20 KT...GUSTS  30 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0000Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 24.1N 126.8W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 20/0900Z
 
$$
FORECASTER D. ZELINSKY
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Elida Forecast Discussion Number 22
item.description:
Issued at 800 PM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026 
000
WTPZ45 KNHC 200241
TCDEP5
 
Tropical Storm Elida Discussion Number  22
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP052026
800 PM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026
 
Convection associated with Elida has continued to weaken and its 
low-level center is now exposed. The initial intensity estimate has 
been lowered slightly to 45 kt, based on a blend of the latest 
Final-T and CI Dvorak estimate from TAFB and the expectation that 
the tropical storm has weakened slightly since partial ASCAT data 
earlier today showed peak winds near 45 kt. Further gradual 
weakening is expected as Elida continues to move over cooler waters 
and into a drier and more stable environment. Increasing 
southwesterly wind shear should also contribute to weakening. The 
cyclone is now forecast to become a remnant low within 24 to 36 h, 
which is supported by simulated satellite forecasts from several 
dynamical models. However, if the recent weakening trend for 
convection associated with the tropical storm to continues 
overnight, that transition could occur even sooner.

Elida appears to be very slightly farther west than previously 
estimated, but it is still expected to make a turn toward the north 
tonight and pick up forward speed as it turns between a deep-layer 
trough to its northwest and a ridge centered over the southwestern 
U.S. Only a very slight westward adjustment was made to the track 
forecast to account for the tweak in the initial position. The 
reliable guidance is in good agreement on Elida's track, and the 
tropical storm is expected to generally move northward for the next 
day or so until it becomes a remnant low. The remnants are then 
expected to continue northward and gradually slow down until the 
cyclone dissipates near the middle of the week.

 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  20/0300Z 24.1N 126.8W   45 KT  50 MPH
 12H  20/1200Z 26.0N 127.3W   40 KT  45 MPH
 24H  21/0000Z 28.7N 127.6W   35 KT  40 MPH
 36H  21/1200Z 31.6N 127.7W   30 KT  35 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 48H  22/0000Z 34.2N 127.6W   25 KT  30 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 60H  22/1200Z 35.6N 127.4W   20 KT  25 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 72H  23/0000Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster D. Zelinsky
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

LATITUDE 24.1 NORTH...LONGITUDE 126.8 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED   
WINDS NEAR 45 KTS...50 MPH...85 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        00Z MON AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -

  TIME       00Z MON 12Z MON 00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 00Z THU 00Z FRI
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             12Z MON 00Z TUE 12Z TUE 00Z WED 00Z THU 00Z FRI 00Z SAT
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
25N 125W       34 83   X(83)   X(83)   X(83)   X(83)   X(83)   X(83)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER D. ZELINSKY
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
